source_paper_title: Inexact Bregman Proximal Gradient Method and Its Inertial Variant with Absolute and Partial Relative Stopping Criteria
context:
Page 9, Section 3 (discussion immediately after Algorithm 1, comparing (AbSC) vs (ReSC))

Although this requirement can be satisfied in many cases (see two examples in the following subsection), it may limit the applicability of (ReSC). Note that Bello-Cruz et al. [18] recently incorporated a full relative-type error rule in the FISTA, which can accommodate a nonzero error \(\Delta_k\). Thus, it would be interesting to explore whether the requirement \(\|\Delta_k\| = 0\) can be removed in our methods, which we will leave for future research.
title: Allow nonzero optimality-residual error \(\Delta_k\) in the relative-type stopping criterion for inexact Bregman proximal gradient methods
background_latex:
Let \(E\) be a finite-dimensional Euclidean space and \(Q\subseteq E\) a closed convex set with nonempty interior. Consider the convex composite minimization problem \(\min_{x\in Q} F(x):=P(x)+f(x)\), where \(P:E\to(-\infty,+\infty]\) is proper closed convex, and \(f:E\to\mathbb{R}\) is convex and differentiable on \(\mathrm{int}(Q)\). Let \(\phi:E\to(-\infty,+\infty]\) be an essentially smooth, strictly convex kernel with \(\mathrm{dom}(\phi)=Q\), and define the Bregman distance \(D_\phi(u,v):=\phi(u)-\phi(v)-\langle\nabla\phi(v),u-v\rangle\) for \(v\in\mathrm{int}(Q)\). Fix \(\lambda>L\), where \(L\ge 0\) is a relative smoothness constant of \(f\) with respect to \(\phi\) (restricted to an appropriate closed convex set \(X\supseteq \mathrm{dom}(P)\cap Q\)). At iteration \(k\), the inexact Bregman proximal gradient method approximately solves the subproblem
\[
\min_{x\in Q}\; P(x)+\langle \nabla f(x_k),x-x_k\rangle+\lambda D_\phi(x,x_k).
\]
An approximate pair \((x_{k+1},\tilde x_{k+1})\) with an error pair \((\Delta_k,\delta_k)\in E\times\mathbb{R}_+\) is accepted if
\[
\Delta_k\in \partial_{\delta_k}P(\tilde x_{k+1})+\nabla f(x_k)+\lambda(\nabla\phi(x_{k+1})-\nabla\phi(x_k)),
\]
where \(\partial_{\delta}P(z)\) is the \(\delta\)-subdifferential of \(P\) at \(z\).
The paper studies a partial relative-type stopping criterion (ReSC) of the form
\[
\|\Delta_k\|=0,\qquad \lambda^{-1}\delta_k + D_\phi(\tilde x_{k+1},x_{k+1})\le \sigma\, D_\phi(\tilde x_{k+1},x_k),
\]
with a parameter \(\sigma\in[0,1)\).
problem_definition_latex:
Problem: Remove the restriction \(\|\Delta_k\|=0\) in the relative-type stopping criterion (ReSC) while retaining provable convergence guarantees for the resulting inexact Bregman proximal gradient method.

More precisely, design and analyze a verifiable relative-type stopping rule that allows \(\|\Delta_k\|>0\) together with
\[
\Delta_k\in \partial_{\delta_k}P(\tilde x_{k+1})+\nabla f(x_k)+\lambda(\nabla\phi(x_{k+1})-\nabla\phi(x_k)),
\]
and an inequality comparing \(\lambda^{-1}\delta_k + D_\phi(\tilde x_{k+1},x_{k+1})\) to a relative quantity such as \(D_\phi(\tilde x_{k+1},x_k)\), and then prove function-value convergence (e.g., \(F(\tilde x_k)\to \inf_{x\in Q}F(x)\)) and a sublinear rate (e.g., \(F(\tilde x_k)-\inf_{x\in Q}F(x)=O(1/k)\)) under appropriate assumptions on \(f,P,\phi\) and the allowed magnitudes of \(\Delta_k\) and \(\delta_k\).
